fields.description:
# Plan-review verification gate (open→in_progress)

## Context

rebar has a completion-verifier *close* gate (verifies a ticket's work was actually done before close). It has
no equivalent at the *start* of work: when an agent claims a ticket (open→in_progress), nothing checks that the
ticket's implementation plan is sound before the agent executes it. Flawed plans (too big to one-shot, ambiguous,
wrong file targets, ignored consumers, missing tests, criteria that don't match the work, children that don't
cover the parent, unvalidated novel architecture) get executed anyway, and early errors compound over autonomous
agent trajectories (~44% of agent failures are spec/plan defects).

This epic builds a **plan-review verification gate** that fires at open→in_progress — the inverse of the
completion-verifier. It is a rebar FEATURE used by a BROAD, DIVERSE CLIENT BASE (many languages, frameworks,
project structures, and ticket-creation styles), so it must be polyglot, fail-open on unsupported stacks, and
flexible about inconsistently-scoped tickets. Its posture is to **COACH** agents to author better plans via
positive, well-formed guidance — not to nitpick or roadblock — while emitting a signed **certification** that a
review process was followed (a composable "rigorous agentic development vs vibe-coding" signal for CI).

Full design detail lives in session log `63cc-ec94-4792-464b` (plan-of-record v4 + the ~30 research reports +
the discussion narrative). This description is the self-contained epic summary.

## Design summary

- **Trigger / enforcement:** fires at open→in_progress. `claim` is a FAST signature-verify check only (no LLM, no
  network round-trip; target p95 < ~50ms). The heavy review runs OUT-OF-BAND via a dedicated entrypoint
  (`rebar review-plan <ticket>` / an MCP `run_workflow` tool, mirroring `rebar verify-completion`), triggered by
  the agent on a claim-block or by CI. A REVIEW PROCESS, not a dialog — the client revises and re-runs to earn a
  fresh signature (mirrors completion-verifier). Routing pivots on **has_children + complexity + ticket LEVEL/TYPE** (proportionate scrutiny — see below), NOT ticket type alone.
- **Two layers:** LAYER 1 = a minimal, deterministic, structural floor that BLOCKS (frozen, unambiguous, cheap,
  polyglot-where-tooling-exists, fail-open; convergent by construction). LAYER 2 = an ADVISORY coaching review
  (per-criterion judgment + one bounded broad open-ended pass) that SURFACES findings authored for remediation
  and never blocks by default.
- **Verdict model — THREE-PASS (evidence → binary-verify → deterministic gate; see the dedicated section
  below):** the model does NOT emit severity or confidence. Pass 1 surfaces grounded FINDINGS; a SEPARATE Pass-2
  verifier emits severity attributes + atomic binary sub-answers; a DETERMINISTIC Pass 3 computes confidence +
  severity and decides `block | advisory | dropped`. A review-level INDETERMINATE remains for budget/tool-unavailable.
  Defaults ship advisory in v1, but blocking is NOT DET-only: the DET floor blocks structurally AND each
  LLM-tier criterion can block when a project opts it in / sets its `block_threshold` (we ship thresholds
  HIGH so almost everything is advisory during calibration). Per-criterion blocking + a `--force`-override
  are project-configurable. This REPLACES the earlier per-criterion PASS/AMBIGUOUS/FAIL+model-severity model (adopted from
  the shared three-pass framework, epic `9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b`).
- **Coaching / feedback spec:** the reviewer SUPPLIES the finding + location the agent can't self-detect
  (research: LLMs can't FIND their own errors but CAN fix them once located). Each advisory finding is grounded
  in a criterion/file/section, specific + actionable, severity-ranked + capped, affirms what already passes, a
  suggested fix only when confident, expressed as a checklist the agent addresses.
- **Criteria registry:** each criterion is a declarative descriptor carrying `exec` (DET | 1-TURN | 2-STEP |
  AGENT) + `facet` (the concern/context it examines) + `context_needs` + `routing` + `trigger`; a generic
  chunker reads these (reuse rebar.llm's catalog/contracts/prompt/runner) to bucket by tier and pack same-facet
  criteria into chunks — no hand-maintained chunk lists. Frozen within a review loop (convergence), grown between versions.
- **Polyglot tooling (fail-open IRONCLAD):** Layer-1 wraps external tools, ALL added as NEW integrations by this
  epic (none are wired into rebar today): LSP resolution via solidlsp (reachable through the repo's configured
  Serena MCP server, but NOT yet a rebar library) + ast-grep (resolution/callers); syft + per-ecosystem registry +
  LLM-fallback (deps); scc + lizard (size). Unsupported lang / no server / crash / timeout → skipped = PASS, never FAIL.
- **Execution (exec-tier-aware):** DET criteria run as parallel code (~free). SINGLE-TURN criteria are the only
  chunked tier — packed by `facet` at `base_chunk(model) × size_factor(ticket)` (config-overridable; default Opus
  ~all-in-one cached call, Sonnet 6 coherent / 3 plain, halved for large/epic/has_children). AGENT (tool-using)
  criteria run ONE agent each (≈85× a single-turn call) and are gated by proportionate scrutiny. Prompt caching on
  (system+plan prefix) makes fine chunking cheap. Depth scales with complexity + has_children; escalation by
  size + complexity + centrality (computed at plan time). Per-plan budget cap, chunk-atomic checkpointing;
  INDETERMINATE on cap-hit sheds AGENT/overlay criteria first. Defaults grounded by experiment (see below).
- **Proportionate scrutiny (by LEVEL + TYPE; experiment-grounded round 4):** criteria carry an `applies_at` field; the orchestrator filters before chunking so leaf-implementation criteria (E6 proving-commands, G1G2/E4/A1, leaf overlays T5a/b/c) do NOT run at epic/story altitude, container criteria (G3/G4 child coverage) run only when has_children, and TYPE rules apply (bugs EXEMPT→follow-on; E5 suppressed on RED/GREEN test tasks; F4 N/A on internal/mechanical leaf tasks). Without this the gate over-fires on mid-level stories and mechanical tasks.
- **Overlay triggering (deterministic-where-low-FP, else LLM-router; validated round 4):** T1 prior-art, T5a performance, and T5d accessibility fire from low-FP deterministic signals (bright-lines / UI-keyword count); T8 LLM-antipatterns, T6 UX, T5b reliability, T5c security, T9 shared-state are LLM-routed (a keyword trigger is high-FP for them — e.g. T8 keyword-matches almost every LLM ticket). T8 is in the active set for LLM/agent-design plans (it recovers structural-gap signal a generic checklist misses). FINALIZE CORRECTION (E4-confirmed): the new resource overlays T10/T11/T12 are LLM-ROUTED for PLAN review, NOT deterministic — on a held-out non-DSO corpus the deterministic keyword over-fired (rebar 'migration' = the bash→Python strangler, not a data-migration: det T11 fired 2/19 plans, all false; T12 'deploy'/'rollback' fired 7/19, all false; the LLM router fired 0). On a code-review DIFF the file-glob triggers (the 9da1 overlay catalog) are high-precision; on a PLAN there is no diff, so route by LLM.
- **Attestation:** signed result = "process followed to the best of our ability, no red flags + coverage" — NOT
  "perfect". Bound to the latest MATERIAL event commit (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition edits invalidate;
  tags/comments/links/assignee do not). The cert is the composable CI rigor signal.
- **Observability / FP substrate (cheap, in-scope):** a reducer-IGNORED SIDECAR `REVIEW_RESULT` event on the
  ticket (out of hot paths) capturing per-criterion verdicts + finding fingerprints + metadata + override
  justifications; the signature stays a normal head-state event. Offline replay+join (ticket_id + fingerprints +
  existing EDIT/STATUS/completion-verification events) yields per-criterion fire/remediation/FP/FN rates. Capture
  only; analysis is offline; no human-feedback requirement.
- **Bugs are SKIPPED** in this epic (different shape; dedicated follow-on).

## Review structure — three-pass (evidence → binary-verify → deterministic gate)

Adopted from epic `9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b` (the shared three-pass framework for all rebar reviews) + the
code-review plan-of-record (session log `9dba-8e84-0d0e-4af8`). It **replaces per-criterion model-emitted
severity/confidence**: LLMs are unreliable at holistic severity/confidence, so neither is emitted by the model
anywhere in the decision path. The criteria registry below is the LOCKED RUBRIC the Pass-1 findings reference;
each descriptor's prose `SEVERITY:` text is now a PRIOR for the Pass-2 attribute assessment, not a model output.

- **Pass 1 — find (evidence records):** the reviewer surfaces FINDINGS as
  `{finding, criteria[] (rubric ids), evidence[] (flexible: plan quote / section / ABSENCE rationale / code
  citation), scenarios[], impact}` — NO severity, NO confidence. Run per facet-chunk of the rubric (the chunking
  defaults still apply); PASS = no finding for a criterion; an empty list for a clean chunk is expected.
- **Pass 2 — verify (independent):** a SEPARATE verifier agent, fresh context, re-grounds in the plan (and the
  code AGENTICALLY for codebase-grounded findings) and emits per finding (a) severity ATTRIBUTES
  `{prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility}` (coarse ordinals) and (b) typed BINARY
  sub-answers `{verdict: yes|no|insufficient}`. Rules: atomicity (one proposition/question); INDEPENDENCE (the
  finding is presented as a CLAIM TO TEST, its conclusion not asserted); verdict-with-citation NOT verdict-with-fix;
  `insufficient` allowed.
- **Pass 3 — decide (deterministic):** the ONLY veto is cited-code-reference-accuracy, applied ONLY when a finding
  cites a specific code reference (a no-op for the non-citable absence findings that dominate plan review).
  confidence = graded fraction of the binary sub-answers (yes=1, insufficient=.5, no=0); severity = computed from
  the attributes; decision = `block | advisory | dropped` (drop < .5 confidence) against per-criterion
  `block_threshold`. Defaults START HIGH so almost every LLM criterion is **advisory** during calibration — but
  an LLM-tier criterion DOES block when a project opts it in / lowers its threshold; blocking is not DET-only. No model holistic severity/confidence in the path; no interactive
  escalation (autonomous).
- **Pass-2 vocabulary (validated against `criteria_v8`).** Severity ATTRIBUTES: `prod_impact` / `debt_impact` ∈ {none, low, medium, high}; `blast_radius` ∈ {local, module, system}; `likelihood` ∈ {low, medium, high}; `reversibility` ∈ {easy, moderate, hard}. BINARY sub-answers ∈ {yes, no, insufficient}: the VETO `cited_reference_accurate` (answered only when a code citation is present, else `na`) + the graded set {`is_verifiable`, `evidence_entails_finding`, `path_reachable`, `impact_follows_necessarily`, `no_viable_alternative_explanation`, `no_existing_mitigation`, `severity_claim_justified`}. Pass-3 maps the attributes → severity and the graded fraction → confidence.
- **Binary sub-question set (typed):** veto = cited-reference-accuracy; graded = is-verifiable,
  evidence-entails-finding, path-reachable, impact-follows-necessarily, no-viable-alternative-explanation,
  no-existing-mitigation, severity-claim-justified. (Research: Chain-of-Verification, FactScore/SAFE,
  RAGAS-faithfulness, TICK — binary decomposition + deterministic aggregation beats a single LLM confidence number.)
- **Plan-review specialization:** evidence is flexible precisely because plan findings are often NON-citable
  (an absence finding like “the plan never specifies X” has no line to cite); the cited-reference veto is therefore
  a no-op for them and fires only when a finding does cite code.

The three-pass-native descriptor set is `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/criteria_v8.json`
(`severity_by=pass3`, `default_posture=advisory`, `block_threshold`, `checklist[]`, declarative `applies_at`).

## Criteria registry (authoritative full set — v4 §5; canonical)

The complete criteria set. This list is the authority; the experiment subsets are not. A completeness guard
(`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/check_registry_coverage.py`) fails loudly if any criterion here is
missing from the implemented set, so subsetting can never silently drop one again.

**Layer 1 — DET (deterministic, blocks, fail-open):** P1 readiness-shape (`## Acceptance Criteria`, all types) ·
P2 file/symbol/import resolution (LSP/ast-grep) · P3 package existence (syft+registry+LLM-fallback) · P4 oversize
signals · P5 task-DAG validity + cross-task interference · P6 AC/DD quality (compound-AND, vague-lexicon,
spec-fidelity, verify-command robustness) · P7 destructive/irreversible sniff (escalates to T4).

**Layer 2 — judgment (advisory; single-turn / 2-step / AGENT):** F1 measurability & in-session completability ·
F4 user/problem present · E1 criteria↔description coherence + terminology + duplicates · E2 ambiguity +
undercooked + decision-deferral · E3 intent fidelity (2-step blind-restate) · E5 testing-plan completeness ·
E6 verification/termination + end-state-reachability · G5 decomposition judgment · G1+G2 edit-set/scope accuracy
(AGENT) · E4 assumption/premise verification (AGENT) · A1 anti-slop/NIH + the 7 anti-patterns (AGENT) · G3 child coverage (single-turn, container) · G4 child consistency (single-turn, container) · G6 approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection (AGENT; mechanism-correctness + best-approach check, with reviewer-generated alternatives so no rejected options reach the implementer) · ISF intent-source fidelity (single-turn/2-STEP, FED the linked session log + ticket graph, NOT agent; fires ONLY when the ticket is linked to a session log; catches silent descoping of an externally-expressed requirement). (Final AGENT tier = E4/G1G2/A1/G6/T8/T1/T3/T5c/T10/T11 — settled by the grounding experiment: a verdict that depends on WHAT THE ACTUAL CODE DOES is agent-tier (T5c security, T10 infra found a real committed secret, T11 migration by analogy/untested). Single-turn implication overlays whose verdict is plan-text-evident: T5a/T5b/T5e/T9/T4 (T5a/T5b/T11 flagged for eval re-test). G3/G4 single-turn ticket-analysis.)

**Triggered overlays (deterministic where low-FP — T1/T5a/T5d; else LLM-routed — T8/T6/T5b/T5c/T9):** T1
prior-art/novel-architecture · T2 empirical probe (red→green / spike) · T3 integration feasibility · T4
compat/destructiveness-as-explicit-choice · T5a performance · T5b reliability · T5c security · T5d accessibility ·
T5e maintainability · T6 UX non-happy-path · T7 documentation · T8 LLM/prompt antipatterns · T9 shared-state lifecycle (+ concurrency/idempotency) · T10 infrastructure/IaC · T11 data-migration/backfill safety · T12 rollout/rollback/reversibility.

**Cross-cutting:** COH coherence pass (cross-section contradictions) · BROAD bounded open-ended pass
(unknown-unknowns) · benign-reading FP filter · anti-fabrication. Routing/exec/facet/checklist per criterion live in the CURRENT descriptor set (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/criteria_v8.json` — 32 descriptors incl. ISF; v5/v6 are superseded historical references kept only in the reconciliation note below). Round-5 roll-ins extended A1 (7 anti-patterns), T9 (concurrency/TOCTOU), T5a (cost), T5b (observability + degraded-mode), T5c (least-privilege + secrets), E5 (change-detector tests), T4 (rollback-as-remedy), P3 (license/CVE/health), G5 (consume P4 + vertical-slice MVP sequencing). Coverage vs 2025-2026 LLM-plan failure-mode research + full gap analysis: docs/experiments/plan-review-criteria-coverage-and-gaps.md.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Claiming a ticket (open→in_progress) requires a valid, non-stale signed plan-review attestation; absence or
      a material-edit-invalidated signature blocks the claim, with a recorded `--force` override available.
- [ ] The deterministic Layer-1 floor blocks only on sound, unambiguous checks and fails open on any unsupported
      language/tool (skipped = pass), with coverage recorded on the attestation.
- [ ] Layer-2 review returns coaching findings that are structurally well-formed — each carries criterion id,
      file/section location, severity, a checklist line, and a suggested fix when confident — verifiable in-session
      against the REVIEW_RESULT schema; judgment criteria are advisory by default. (Remediation-rate uplift is
      tracked OFFLINE as follow-on F1, not an in-session gate.)
- [ ] A project can configure risk posture, chunking, budget caps, and per-criterion blocking via config, with
      sensible defaults shipped, INCLUDING a config PREFERENCE that turns the plan-review requirement ON/OFF
      for claiming a ticket or transitioning it to in_progress. The preference DEFAULTS OFF when unspecified;
      'rollback' is simply turning the preference off — an ordinary config preference, not a deploy-time
      kill-switch, and no separate rollback mechanism is needed.
- [ ] Every review emits a `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar event sufficient to reconstruct per-criterion FP/remediation
      analysis offline, without taxing rebar's hot paths.
- [ ] The signed attestation is checkable by an external CI process as a rigor/anti-vibe-coding signal.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Layer-1 deterministic criteria implemented in Python, polyglot, fail-open: readiness shape (standardized
      `## Acceptance Criteria` across all types), file/symbol/import resolution (LSP/ast-grep), package existence
      (syft + registry + fallback), oversize signals, task-DAG validity, AC/DD quality, destructive sniff.
- [ ] Layer-2 advisory criteria implemented: measurability/agent-completable, user/problem present, criteria↔
      description coherence, ambiguity, intent fidelity (2-step blind-restate), testing-plan completeness,
      verification/termination, edit-set/scope accuracy (tool-primed), child coverage + consistency (containers),
      assumption/premise verification, anti-slop, and T2 empirical-probe (the design's existing empirical-validation overlay: a complex/novel plan with an unvalidated assumption should plan a spike/probe/measurement — SUPPRESSED when the ticket already includes experimentation; LLM-routed); plus triggered overlays (deterministic or LLM-routed) and a bounded broad pass.
- [ ] Three-pass verdict model implemented (the model emits NO holistic severity/confidence in the decision path): Pass-1 emits evidence records {finding, criteria[], evidence[], scenarios[], impact} with no severity/confidence; a SEPARATE Pass-2 verifier (single aggregate call, non-frontier model) emits severity attributes {prod/debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility} + the typed binary sub-question set {yes|no|insufficient}; a DETERMINISTIC Pass-3 aggregator computes confidence (graded fraction) + severity (from attributes) and decides block|advisory|dropped against per-criterion thresholds. Adopted from epic 9da1.
- [ ] Criteria registry (declarative descriptors) + generic orchestrator, reusing rebar.llm extension points.
- [ ] Attestation: signing + material-event-commit binding + invalidation-on-material-edit + coverage record,
      reusing the close-gate signing machinery (HMAC under the MCP environment key) — integrity/forgery/replay/
      staleness model inherited, no new key custody.
- [ ] `claim` integration: fast signed-attestation check with `--force`+justification override.
- [ ] Configurable chunking; proportionate-depth routing (complexity + has_children); escalation by
      size+complexity+centrality (centrality/blast-radius computed at plan time); per-plan budget cap with
      chunk-atomic checkpointing and an INDETERMINATE outcome (non-blocking default, configurable).
- [ ] Feedback-authoring conforms to the coaching spec (grounded/specific/ranked/affirming/checklist).
- [ ] `REVIEW_RESULT` reducer-ignored sidecar event with finding fingerprints; reducer skips the type; override
      logging; offline-analysis-ready.
- [ ] Per-criterion config: advisory (default) | blocking-with-override.
- [ ] Bugs are exempt from this gate (routed to a follow-on).
- [x] Defaults for chunking + budget + depth grounded in the experiment results (see `## Experiment-grounded defaults` below + docs/experiments/).
- [ ] Tests + verification: every AC maps to a concrete proving check; fail-open is tested per tool (unsupported-
      lang / no-server / crash / timeout → skipped=pass), plus tests for signature invalidation-on-material-edit,
      `--force` override logging, cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding (agent/overlay-first), and the reducer-ignored
      REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (reducer skips the type; head/.cache/deps/validate unaffected).
- [ ] STANDING per-criterion eval suite for the NEW plan-review prompts (this gate's criteria): a labeled fixture set wired into the workflow-engine eval seam (promptfoo/Inspect), drawing sample plans from all three corpuses — DSO (read-only), snap-oakhart-manual (Rails/Ruby), and rebar's own internal tickets (Python) — with recall (bad→finding), false-accept (bad→pass), and false-fire (good→finding) cases per criterion, human-adjudicated. Every prompt/criterion change is eval-gated against it (calibrate to the graded sample — Who-Validates-the-Validators / Rulers). Promotes F10 into scope; the one-shot E1 (seeded recall) + E4 (corpus generalization) experiments SEED it but the suite lives in the prompt library / eval seam, not `docs/experiments/`. Open: weak auto-labels (closed tickets + completion-verification verdicts) vs. human grading. (The eval SEAM infrastructure + eval coverage for the EXISTING migrated reviewer prompts live in the remediation epic 6f2d; this AC owns the NEW-prompt eval set.)
- [ ] End-to-end proving check: an automated test exercises the full flow — claim-block (absent/stale signature) → run review-plan → earn a signature → claim succeeds — AND its EDGE cases (--force override + logging; material-edit invalidation; fail-open on an unsupported stack; cap-hit INDETERMINATE; the gate-off config preference leaving `claim` at today's behavior).
- [ ] Instrumentation/logging: the gate records per-pass latency + cost (the fast claim-path check and the out-of-band review) so latency/cost targets are set initially from UX/timeout projections and refined PASSIVELY from dogfood data — no upfront benchmark step required. Captured via the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar metadata.
- [ ] Docs: how the gate works, the config surface, fail-open behavior, the cert semantics, and the CI-check pattern.

## Experiment-grounded defaults (round 3; config-overridable)

Grounded by 5 experiment rounds (~1,600 LLM runs). Full design: docs/experiments/plan-review-criteria-storage-and-chunking.md.

- **Execution tiers are the primary axis (not chunk size).** DET (P1–P7) = parallel code, ~free. SINGLE-TURN
  (F1,F4,E2,E5,G5,T5a–e) = chunkable LLM calls, ~$0.005–0.008/criterion cached. 2-STEP (E1,E3,E6) = two ordered
  calls. AGENT/tool-using (E4,G1G2,A1,T1,T6–T8) = one agent loop per criterion, ~$0.12 + ~50s each (≈85× a
  single-turn call) — NOT chunkable; gated by proportionate scrutiny.
- **Criteria storage:** each descriptor declares `exec` (tier), `facet` (the concern/context it examines — the
  advisory grouping key), `context_needs`, `routing`, `trigger`. The chunker is generic: filter → bucket by tier
  → pack same-`facet` single-turn criteria up to the chunk size → one agent per AGENT criterion → DET as code.
  Groupings are advisory (facet tags re-pack at any chunk size); adding/tuning a criterion only declares exec+facet.
- **Chunk size = base_chunk(model) × size_factor(ticket), within the single-turn tier.** base_chunk = {opus 12,
  sonnet 6 with coherent facet packing (3 without), haiku/local 3}; size_factor = {trivial/moderate 1.0,
  large|epic|has_children 0.5}; clamp [2, n]. → Opus packs ~all single-turn criteria in one cached call (its
  consistency is flat in N); Sonnet 6 (coherent) / 3 (large); never the full set on the largest plans.
- **Enable prompt caching** (system+plan as the cached prefix): collapses the cost gap between chunk sizes (1.55×
  vs 2.6×), so chunk for reliability not cost. Cached fine-chunk Sonnet (~$0.07–0.09/review) ≈ one Opus pass
  (~$0.10) — prefer Opus for max consistency/big-plan stability, Sonnet+cache+coherent for the cost floor.
- **Budget (generous, per-plan):** cap = $0.15 (DET+single-turn base) + n_agentic_fired × $0.25 + headroom (small
  task ~$0.15–0.40; heavy epic ~$1.5–2.0 + minutes). INDETERMINATE on cap-hit sheds lowest-priority AGENT/overlay
  criteria first (85× cost), never the cheap base.
- **Criteria are fully specified from DSO source** (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/dso-criteria-specs.md), not one-liners,
  and the set is >12 (single-turn ~13 + agentic + overlays + DET P1–P7).

## Registry reconciliation (post-round-4)

The round-4 'EXP' criterion was the design's existing **T2 empirical probe** reinvented under a wrong name. Reconciling v4 §5 against what was implemented found other dropped criteria: **T3** integration feasibility and **T4** compat/destructiveness (never implemented), **T5d** accessibility (dropped from the criteria set), the dedicated **coherence pass**, and **G3/G4** container child-coverage (designed, never run). All are restored to the corrected criteria set (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/criteria_v5.json) and the full v4 §5 registry is the canonical source. See docs/experiments/criteria-registry-reconciliation.md.

## Design decisions revisited (round 5; SETTLED in the brainstorm)

A round-5 external review re-raised two design questions — but both were already the brainstorm's central debates (session log 63cc, DISCUSSION NARRATIVE + final-decisions entry), resolved with explicit reasoning; they are NOT open.
(1) **Blocking on `claim` vs coaching** — the brainstorm explicitly weighed A blocking-inline / B advisory / C decoupled-signed-attestation and chose **C**: `claim` is a FAST succeed/fail check for a signed 'ready' attestation, the heavy review runs out-of-band, `--force`+justification is the escape. The 'hard-gate-is-anti-pattern' research was examined and found NOT to transfer to autonomous agents (which confabulate rather than ask, and where early errors compound; separate-critic >> self-critique). The minimal sound DET floor is the only hard blocker; Layer-2 never blocks. (2) **What the signature certifies** — an Opus adversarial review in the brainstorm raised the same 'fail-open hides a hollow signal' critique; the resolution is that the cert means 'process followed, no red flags' (NOT 'perfect') WITH **coverage recorded**, the rich per-criterion verdicts/fingerprints live in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar, and a project composes any hard CI gate by checking the signed result + coverage. The brainstorm also explicitly rejected block-by-default-with-routine-override (>30% FP → tool ignored) in favor of advisory-Layer-2.
The genuinely-useful RESIDUAL from the round-5 review (tuning, not redesign): state end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly (not just the 50ms claim check); the prompt-cache busts on the edit-then-re-review loop (re-derive that saving); and make the CI rigor predicate canonical in-repo rather than leaving each client to define it. Notably the review's findings map to COH (a criterion we'd dropped) and G6 (the one we added) — evidence the registry is on track.

## Self-review note (gate run on this epic, round 3)

The full gate was run on this epic per the default protocol (DET floor pass; Opus single-turn facet-chunks;
Sonnet agentic tier with real rebar-repo tools). It surfaced — and this revision addresses — the false 'already
in repo' tooling claim (E4/G1G2, caught only by the agentic tier), a missing test/verification AC (E5/E6), an
unnamed invocation surface (broad pass), an unmeasurable success criterion (F1), and a missing claim-path latency
target (T5a). The gate's **G5 finding was CORRECT** (round 3): this epic should be decomposed. **RESOLVED — now decomposed into 11 children reflecting the converged three-pass design** (the original 9 were deleted as stale; see the re-decomposition). FP rate
was low (F4/E1/E2/E3/A1 passed); see docs/experiments/ for the baseline assessment.

## Scope carve-outs & related epics

Explicit boundaries — this epic does NOT carry a list of vaguely-scoped follow-on work.

**Out of scope — hypothetical future tuning (NOT tracked).** Speculative until the gate ships and the
`REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar yields real FP/FN data: FP/remediation-rate auto-tuning tooling; auto-demoting a
blocking criterion on an FP ceiling; a confidence/severity blocking floor; promote-to-blocking-by-default; a
rigor-signature chain / CI-helper layer; and severity-minor-floor / NOT-flag tuning. Deliberately not in scope
and not tracked — revisit only with real calibration data.

**Spun out as separate epics (linked):**
- Code-grounding tooling — `8f6c-6008-33f3-4f82` (**prerequisite**; this epic depends_on it).
- Project configuration tool + UX — `55de-25ab-ca20-4c79`.
- Bug-ticket review gate (bugs are exempt here) — `3e50-4905-7830-432f`.
- Project-supplied criteria + project-invariant compliance — `3156-9092-1967-4367`.
- Code-drift invalidation (beyond the material-edit binding) — `4fcd-449a-d1b3-4a23`.

**Handled in this epic.** Calibrated abstention + the labeled gold set = the standing per-criterion eval suite
(see the eval-suite acceptance criterion). Cross-ticket overlap (the former "F7") is largely an EXISTING
capability: `G4` covers scope-overlap / dependency-gap WITHIN an epic's children, and `next_batch` +
`duplicates`/`supersedes` links cover deterministic file-conflict + explicit duplicate links; only SEMANTIC
overlap across UNRELATED store-wide tickets is uncovered — disposition deferred to the brainstorm (separate
epic vs out of scope), not tracked here as a vague follow-on.
